titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sarah Bishop Brigham Papers
titlestmt.author: Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday April 6, 2026 at 23:25

did.unittitle: Sarah Bishop Brigham papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Memoir, photographs, ephemera, and correspondence documenting Sarah Bishop Brigham's journey to Alaska and time at the Swedish mission in Golovin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist primarily of a memoir by Mrs. Brigham about her experiences in Alaska, much of it drafted while she was still at Golovin Bay in the spring of 1899. It describes the Brighams' journey, the Golovin Bay Mission, Alaska Native culture, the gold rush at Cape Nome, the communities of Unalaska, St. Michael, and "Anvil City" in Nome, the Alaska mail service, observations of reindeer, and various people Mrs. Brigham knew during her year there. The papers also include correspondence, some of it regarding mining claims owned by Mrs. Brigham; receipts and ephemera, including a program from and sketch of the Jesse Lee Home in Unalaska; newspaper clippings; drawings; and 38 photographs. Over half the photographs are of the mission community at Golovin Bay, many of them taken by the missionary P. H. Anderson. Also present are several photos of miners at Nome, one of workers on the Alaska Railroad, two by Winter and Pond of a mine at Douglas, and studio portraits of Dr. and Mrs. Brigham taken in Burlington, Vermont, some years before their Alaska trip.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sarah Bishop Brigham and her husband, Dr. C. O. Brigham, of Rutland, Vermont, travelled to Alaska in 1898 with the Vermont Klondike Expedition. Dr. Brigham died of an illness at Golovin Bay. Mrs. Brigham taught in the school run by the Swedish mission at Golovin Bay until the journey home was possible in July of 1899.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Morrill and Son on the William Robertson Coe Fund, 1956.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Folders 63-65: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sarah Bishop Brigham Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1898-1903. II. Writings, 1899 and undated. III. Other papers, 1898-1907. IV. Visual materials, 1897-1899. V. Restricted Fragile, 1898-1907.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was previously described with a short catalog record. A retroactive finding aid was created in 2026 to facilitate description and access to the collection at the folder level.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Some folder titles contain language which may be inaccurate and harmful to Alaska Natives. Original titles have been maintained to preserve the original context of how the collection creator labeled their documents. Please contact Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library for details or if you have questions or comments about these revisions. For more information on reparative archival description at Yale, see Yale's Statement on Harmful Language in Archival Description.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Letters from the Brighams are filed first. Other letters are listed alphabetically by correspondent.

did.unittitle: 3 letters from Dr. Charles O. Brigham to Lynn Brigham
4 letter fragments from Sarah Bishop Brigham to various recipients en route to Seattle and at St. Michaels
Sarah Bishop Brigham letter to the Swedish missionary society regarding the Golovin Bay Mission School
Alaska Commercial Company letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham regarding her claim on Dry Creek
2 letters from Mrs. O. P. Anderson to Sarah Bishop Brigham
P. H. Anderson letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Harriet M. Butterfield letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Charles S. D. Emerson letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
George K. Griffin letter and postal forwarding instructions to Sarah Bishop Brigham
3 letters from J. H. James and his agent James Howell to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Amanda Johnson letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
"Mrs. King" letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Mrs. C. W. Lane letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
3 letters from Alice Omegitjoak to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Rutland Herald letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham regarding her manuscript
Stephen S. Smith letter to Sarah Bishop Brigham
Letter from an unidentified correspondent to Sarah Bishop Brigham regarding her mining claims on Anvil Creek and Dry Creek
Writings
Manuscript draft
Typescript drafts of Chapters 1 and 2
Manuscript draft of chapter describing Dr. Brigham's death and hardships in Alaska
Manuscript draft of "Eskimos" chapter

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This folder title contains language that may be inaccurate or harmful to Alaska Native communities. Original folder titles have been maintained to preserve the original context of how the collection creator labeled their files.

did.unittitle: Manuscript draft of "Mrs. J. H. Bings" section
Manuscript draft of "C. C. Reynolds" section
Manuscript drafts of "Dr. Gleaver" and "Kotzebue Sound" sections
Manuscript drafts of "A Trip to the Mines of Camp Nome" section
Manuscript drafts of a chapter about mining
Manuscript drafts of "The Mail Service of Alaska 98-99" section
Manuscript drafts of "Reindeer Herd" section
Manuscript draft and diary pages for "Anvil City" chapter
Manuscript drafts of "Finale" chapter
Miscellaneous loose pages, including a description of the Brighams' journey to St. Michaels
Other writings
"Eskimo Brotherhood" manuscript and typescript drafts

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This folder title contains language that may be inaccurate or harmful to Alaska Native communities. Original folder titles have been maintained to preserve the original context of how the collection creator labeled their files.

did.unittitle: "Harnessing the Reindeer" manuscript and typescript drafts
"New Gold Finds or Fields North of Norton Sound" manuscript drafts
"Opening remarks" manuscript drafts
"Unalaska" manuscript draft
Other papers
Receipt, letter of identification, and purser's ticket from the SS Brixham
Business and calling cards
Blank Vermont Klondike Expedition contract and receipt
Manuscript map of western Alaska and sketch of an unidentified Alaska Native
Printed program and sketch from a musical evening at the Jesse Lee Home, Unalaska
Flyer for Sarah Bishop Brigham's talks on western Alaska at the Congregational Church parsonage, Rutland, Vermont
List of names of Rutland area people
Newspaper clippings about the Brighams and the Vermont Klondike Expedition
Newspaper clippings about Alaska
Klondike Clarion and Unalaska Evangelist poster
Visual material
Studio portraits [possibly of Charles and Sarah Brigham?], L.A. Atwood, Burlington, Vermont
18 photographs of missionary P. H. Anderson of Golovin Bay
2 photographs of children at the mission school and five men on a snowy roof
3 photographs of Golovin Bay, including Charles Brigham's grave, the Brighams' cabin, and the church school
2 photographs of the ice breaking in Golovin Bay
6 photographs of Anvil City, one with a drawing of an unidentified Alaska Native on the verso
2 photographs of the mouth of Snake River
2 photographs of Treadwell Gold Mine and Mill in Douglas, Alaska
Photograph of workers on a railroad in Alaska
6 drawings of Golovin Bay and other coastal scenes
Restricted fragile

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: Original newspaper clippings from Box 1
